name: Apache NiFi Data Flow API
what_it_does: Open-source data flow automation tool with REST API — enabling organizations to design, control, and monitor data flows between systems via a visual programming interface and REST API, with support for 300+ processors (connectors), backpressure handling, data provenance tracking, and fine-grained security for regulated industries, originally created by NSA.
use_cases: Automating data flows via NiFi REST API for programmatic processor and connection management
Monitoring data flow health via NiFi API for pipeline observability and error detection
Managing NiFi templates via REST API for flow template deployment automation
Controlling data routing via NiFi API for dynamic flow configuration updates
Integrating IoT data streams via NiFi for MQTT/OPC-UA edge data collection pipelines
not_for: Cloud-native managed ELT (use Fivetran/Airbyte for managed SaaS ETL)
Large-scale batch processing (use Spark/Flink for massive parallel compute)
Simple source-to-destination pipelines without routing logic
best_when: An organization needs a self-hosted, highly regulated data flow tool with visual programming, provenance tracking, and fine-grained security — particularly government, healthcare, and financial institutions with air-gap or on-premises requirements.
avoid_when: You need cloud-native managed ETL, lack Java operational expertise, or want a simpler no-code pipeline tool.

auth.notes: Multiple auth options: (1) HTTP Basic (username:password) for simple deployments. (2) OIDC/OAuth2 via external IdP (Okta, Keycloak). (3) Client certificates (mTLS). (4) JWT token via POST /nifi-api/access (exchange credentials for token). Token: Authorization: Bearer {jwt_token}. REST API at {host}:8443/nifi-api/. Default: HTTPS required. Unsecured mode: possible but NOT recommended for production. LDAP: enterprise directory integration. Multi-tenant: NiFi Registry for flow version control.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; commercial support from Cloudera (HDF/NiFi) or Hortonworks
pricing.notes: Apache Software Foundation (ASF). Originally created by NSA (National Security Agency) as 'Niagarafiles'. Donated to Apache in 2014. Apache 2.0 license. Java 17+ required. NiFi Registry: version control for flows. MiNiFi: lightweight NiFi for edge/IoT. NiFi Stateless: serverless NiFi for streaming. Cloudera DataFlow: commercial NiFi as managed cloud service. 300+ processors: HTTP, MQTT, Kafka, HDFS, AWS S3, Azure Blob, etc. Data provenance: full lineage tracking for every FlowFile. Backpressure: automatic flow control. GDPR: data provenance is both a compliance asset and a challenge (stores all data paths).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JWT from POST /nifi-api/access. Multiple auth modes. Self-hosted Java. HTTPS required in production. NiFi Registry for version control. NSA origins. FedRAMP-ready. Complex data provenance. 300+ processors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: REST API reads idempotent. Processor/connection creation: check existing by name before creating. Flow template deployment: idempotent by template version. Process group operations: verify state before modifying.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT authentication: POST to /nifi-api/access with credentials to get JWT — short TTL (12 hours default)
HTTPS mandatory: NiFi refuses HTTP connections in secured mode — configure TLS before API access
NiFi API is complex: process groups, processors, connections, ports all have separate APIs
Cluster vs standalone: NiFi cluster APIs include cluster coordination — test against cluster endpoint
Data provenance: NiFi stores metadata for every FlowFile processed — significant storage impact
NiFi Registry: separate service for flow version control — configure separately from NiFi instance

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (HTTPS required in production). mTLS client certificate support. OIDC/OAuth2/LDAP for enterprise IdP integration. NSA design heritage: strong security focus baked in. Data provenance: full audit trail for all data flows — excellent for compliance but GDPR complication. FedRAMP-capable. FIPS mode available. Role-based access control on processors and process groups. Self-hosted: operator controls all security. Sensitive property encryption: AES-256 for stored credentials in flows.
